name: Grilled Chicken with Yogurt Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 cups plain yogurt (use unflavored, all-natural yogurt without additives or stabilizers)
2 tsp dried mint flakes (or 2 tablespoons chopped fresh mint)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place chicken breasts in a single layer in a shallow glass baking dish. Pour lemon juice over the chicken. Turn the chicken twice to coat it well.
2. Using a mortar and pestle, crush garlic and salt together to form a smooth paste.
3. In a separate bowl, whisk together the garlic paste, yogurt, olive oil, mint (fresh or dried), and black pepper.
4. Pour the yogurt mixture over the chicken, turning to coat it evenly with the marinade. Cover and refrigerate for 1 to 2 hours.
5. When ready to grill, remove the chicken pieces from the marinade and set aside. Reserve the marinade for later use. Grill the chicken over medium heat for 5 to 7 minutes on each side (depending on thickness) until thoroughly cooked.
6. While grilling the chicken, transfer the reserved marinade to a small saucepan. Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently, until the mixture comes to a full boil. Boil for 1 minute. Remove from heat.
7. Dissolve cornstarch in 1 tablespoon cold water and stir it into the sauce. Return the pan to the stove and cook for 1 minute over medium heat, stirring constantly, to thicken the sauce.
8. Serve chicken hot with warm yogurt sauce spooned over each serving.
